title: ismelia viscosa
intro: Ismelia viscosa, also known as the sticky stick or sticky bush, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its sticky sap and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Ismelia viscosa is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic agent, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including a tea made from its leaves and stems, or a topical application of its sap to treat skin conditions and wounds.
